title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani garnished with fresh cilantro and cream, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a classic Indian delight.

description: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a globally celebrated Indian dish, cherished for its incredibly rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato-based gravy. Tender pieces of chicken are simmered in this luscious sauce, offering a delightful balance of sweet, tangy, and savory flavors. It is a true culinary masterpiece, perfect for a comforting and satisfying lunch.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes to the pan. Stir in the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer, then add the soaked cashews. Cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to break down.

instructions.title: Blend the Makhani Sauce
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods from the gravy base. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra smooth sauce, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step ensures a silky texture.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the pan with the smooth gravy to low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces and stir gently to combine. Crush the kasoori methi between your palms and add to the gravy. Pour in the heavy cream and stir until just combined. Heat through for 2-3 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream, to prevent curdling.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with a swirl of extra heavy cream and freshly chopped cilantro. Serve your authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice for a truly satisfying Indian lunch experience.

tips: Marinating the chicken for a longer duration, ideally overnight, significantly enhances its tenderness and allows the spices to penetrate deeply, resulting in a more flavorful dish.
For an exceptionally smooth and rich gravy, soak cashews in warm water for at least 15 minutes before blending. You can also add a tablespoon of almond flour to thicken the sauce if desired.
Always add heavy cream at the very end of cooking and avoid bringing the curry to a rolling boil afterward. This prevents the cream from curdling and maintains the sauce's silky texture.
article:
Unlocking the Flavors of India: A Guide to Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

Few dishes capture the heart and palate quite like Butter Chicken, known in its native tongue as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian lunch recipe has transcended borders, charming food enthusiasts worldwide with its rich, creamy, and subtly sweet-yet-spicy profile. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality experience in your own kitchen, or simply want to understand the magic behind one of India's most beloved culinary exports, you've come to the right place. Dive with us into the world of this delectable dish, from its humble origins to the secrets behind its irresistible taste, making it a perfect hearty meal for any lunch spread.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian curry originating from Delhi in the 1950s. Its creation is often credited to Kundan Lal Gujral, the founder of Moti Mahal restaurant. The story goes that leftover tandoori chicken pieces were simmered in a rich tomato-based gravy enriched with butter and cream, giving birth to this culinary marvel. The name "Murgh Makhani" literally translates to "chicken with butter," perfectly encapsulating its essence.

At its core, Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani features succulent pieces of marinated and often grilled or roasted chicken (traditionally tandoori chicken) bathed in a velvety smooth sauce. This sauce is a complex symphony of pureed tomatoes, aromatic spices, ginger, garlic, cashews for thickness, and generous amounts of butter and fresh cream. The result is a vibrant, slightly tangy, and sweet curry with a profound depth of flavor that appeals to almost every palate, including those new to Indian cuisine. It’s a dish celebrated for its balance and comforting warmth, making it an excellent choice for a satisfying midday meal.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken

Crafting an authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients for both the chicken and the sauce. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast), cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste
*   Optional: 1/2 teaspoon tandoori masala for extra flavor

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter (plus more for finishing)
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 kg ripe tomatoes, pureed (or 2 cans x 400g crushed tomatoes)
*   1/2 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   1/2 cup fresh cream (heavy cream)
*   1 teaspoon sugar (or to taste, balances tanginess)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Murgh Makhani

Follow these steps to create a truly unforgettable Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani:

1.  Marinate the Chicken: In a bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for maximum flavor.
2.  Cook the Chicken: You have a few options:
    *   Grill: Thread chicken onto skewers and grill until charred and cooked through.
    *   Pan-fry: Heat a little oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Cook chicken pieces in batches until browned and cooked through.
    *   Bake: Arrange chicken on a baking sheet and bake at 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked. Set aside.
3.  Prepare the Sauce Base: In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt 2 tablespoons butter with 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 8-10 minutes.
4.  Add Aromatics and Spices: Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the pureed tomatoes, turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the oil separates.
5.  Blend the Sauce: Add the soaked cashews to the tomato mixture. Let it cool slightly, then carefully transfer the mixture to a blender. Blend until absolutely smooth. For an ultra-silky sauce, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot.
6.  Finish the Sauce: Return the smooth sauce to the pot over medium-low heat. Stir in the garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, sugar, and an extra tablespoon or two of butter. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
7.  Combine and Serve: Gently fold in the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in the fresh cream. Heat through for another 2-3 minutes, being careful not to boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed. Garnish generously with fresh cilantro.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani at home is within reach with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key**: Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken sits in the yogurt and spices, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Charred Chicken**: While not strictly necessary, imparting a slight char to your chicken (from grilling, broiling, or a very hot pan) adds a smoky depth that elevates the dish significantly, mimicking the tandoor effect.
*   **Smooth Sauce is Paramount**: Blend your sauce until it's incredibly smooth. If you don't have a high-powered blender, straining it through a fine-mesh sieve will remove any lingering bits of onion or cashew, resulting in that signature velvety texture.
*   **Good Quality Tomatoes**: Use ripe, flavorful tomatoes. If using canned, opt for high-quality crushed or diced tomatoes.
*   **Don't Skimp on Butter and Cream**: These are not just for flavor; they contribute to the dish's luxurious texture. Add them at the end to preserve their delicate taste and prevent the cream from splitting.
*   **Kasoori Methi**: This dried fenugreek leaf is a non-negotiable ingredient. Lightly toast it in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding to the sauce. It adds a unique, slightly bitter, and aromatic complexity that defines the dish.
*   **Balance Flavors**: Butter Chicken often has a delicate balance of sweet, tangy, and spicy. Taste your sauce and adjust sugar or a squeeze of lemon juice as needed to hit that perfect equilibrium.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a truly satisfying Indian lunch.

*   **Bread**: The classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread, perfect for scooping up every last bit of the rich sauce. Other excellent choices include tandoori roti, chapati, or paratha.
*   **Rice**: Basmati rice, especially fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) or a simple plain steamed rice, offers a wonderful contrast and helps soak up the delicious gravy.
*   **Sides**: A refreshing cucumber raita (yogurt dip), a simple kachumber salad (diced onion, tomato, cucumber), or a tangy mango pickle can provide a lovely counterpoint to the richness of the curry.

For a complete and authentic Indian lunch experience, serve a generous portion of Butter Chicken alongside your chosen bread and rice, with a small side of raita or salad.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives**: For a meat-free version, replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) to make Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, chickpeas, or mixed vegetables. The cooking method for these ingredients would be similar, perhaps pan-frying paneer until golden before adding to the sauce.
*   **Dairy-Free**: To make it dairy-free, substitute yogurt with a plant-based alternative for marinating. For the sauce, replace butter with a vegan butter substitute and heavy cream with full-fat coconut cream or a cashew cream (cashews blended with water).
*   **Spice Level**: Adjust the amount of red chili powder to suit your preference. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or a chopped green chili. For a milder flavor, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Sweetness**: The sweetness in Butter Chicken usually comes from the tomatoes and a touch of sugar. You can adjust the sugar to taste or even use a small amount of honey or jaggery.
*   **Smoky Flavor without Tandoor**: If you don't have a grill or broiler, you can still impart a smoky flavor. After cooking the chicken, place a small piece of hot charcoal in a small metal bowl inside the pot with the chicken. Drizzle a little ghee over the charcoal, cover immediately, and let it smoke for a few minutes.

Whether you stick to the traditional recipe or experiment with these variations, the core essence of this beloved Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani remains its comforting and exquisite flavor.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's an experience. Its creamy texture, rich flavors, and aromatic spices make it a universally loved Indian lunch recipe that promises satisfaction with every bite. By following these steps and tips, you can bring the authentic taste of this culinary gem right into your own home. So, gather your ingredients, get cooking, and prepare to delight your senses with one of India's most cherished contributions to global cuisine.
